title: French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
subhead: Lawmakers voted through a new law that introduces a broad ban on social media access for children under 15, without specifying a list of targeted platforms. The law also bans mobile phones in high schools.

title: The Chinese AIs threatening Silicon Valley
Why 19 men have been charged in a French mass rapes case
France's environment minister set to resign, after lawmakers reintroduce banned pesticides
subhead: Monique Barbut had voiced her disagreement with an agriculture law, which, among other measures, calls for the reintroduction of two banned pesticides in France. The legislation was adopted by Parliament on Tuesday evening.

title: Israel is considering new operation against Iran, as war escalates with the US
subhead: Directly involved in the initial phase of the conflict with Iran, Israel said it is prepared to conduct 'both defensive and offensive' operations.

title: Life in Kidal, Mali, amid pro-independence Azawad Liberation Front rebels
subhead: Algerian photojournalist Samir Maouche traveled to the Kidal region, controlled by pro-independence fighters from the Azawad Liberation Front (ALF), in the weeks following their April 25 offensive in northern and central Mali.

title: Copenhagen emerges as new pop laboratory
subhead: A new Danish scene, made up mostly of women, has been offering an alternative, minimalist and sensual pop for several years. Many of the musicians, who often trained at the city's conservatory, have taken their music beyond the borders of Scandinavia.

Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhead: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.
